text: Rare earth elements and permanent magnets: An analysis of current markets, future trends and the need for recycling and secondary supplies This study gives an overview of the position of the rare earth elements (REEs) in the global economy and their role in the production of permanent magnets. While permanent magnets are a mixture of a variety of elements, such as neodymium (Nd), praseodymium (Pr), dysprosium (Dy) and cerium (Ce) and smaller amounts of gadolinium (Gd), the magnet sector is by far the largest and the fastest growing part of the rare earths market. Based on studies such as Rare Earths Outlook to 2030, the magnet applications will constitute about 40% of the REEs demand by 2030. The growth of EVs and renewable energy will further increase the magnet demand, resulting in a potential supply – demand imbalance in relation to the critical magnetic materials. Finally, this paper provides an overview of the present supply situation of the main REE consuming countries, i.e. USA, Europe, Japan and China, and presents an argument for the need of advanced recycling and secondary supplies.
